You are exploring a monorepo at /Users/DKS0700123/Projects/homefield. Find and summarize the current React Native icon registry workflow, focusing on why icon registration may require manual client-side steps.

Please do all of the following in one pass:
1) Locate files implementing icon registration/registry/loading in React Native-related code (apps and libs).
2) Identify where icons are consumed and how developers currently add a new icon.
3) Point out any manual registration APIs or mappings (e.g., registerIcon, registry objects, switch maps, manifests).
4) Find existing tests related to icon registry/icons and summarize test coverage gaps.
5) Provide a concise list of key files with what each one does.
6) Suggest 2-3 concrete implementation approaches to improve DX (e.g., auto-discovery, generated registry, lazy loading), considering likely repo tooling (Nx/TS).

1. /** @format */
2. 
3. import React from 'react';
4. import { View, Text, ScrollView, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
5. import { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react';
6. import {
7. 	HmfIcon,
8. 	registerIcons,
9. 	AlertHelpFilled,
10. 	AlertHelpFilled12,
11. 	AlertHelpFilled16,
12. 	AlertHelpFilled20,
13. 	AlertHelpFilled32,
14. 	AlertHelpFilled40,
15. 	AlertHelpFilled48
16. } from '../../..';
17. 
18. // Register alert-help-filled with its per-size SVG variants
19. registerIcons([
20. 	[
21. 		'alert-help-filled',
22. 		AlertHelpFilled,
23. 		{
24. 			12: AlertHelpFilled12,
25. 			16: AlertHelpFilled16,
26. 			20: AlertHelpFilled20,
27. 			32: AlertHelpFilled32,
28. 			40: AlertHelpFilled40,
29. 			48: AlertHelpFilled48
30. 		}
31. 	]
32. ]);
33. 
34. const RegistrationShowcase = () => (
35. 	<ScrollView style={styles.container}>
36. 		<View style={styles.section}>
37. 			<Text style={styles.sectionTitle}>Size Variant Resolution</Text>
38. 			<Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#666', marginBottom: 12 }}>
39. 				Each size resolves to a dedicated SVG optimized for that pixel size.
40. 			</Text>
41. 			<View style={styles.row}>
42. 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
43. 					<HmfIcon
44. 						name="alert-help-filled"
45. 						size="xxsm"
46. 						color="#000"
47. 					/>
48. 					<Text style={styles.label}>xxsm (12)</Text>
49. 				</View>
50. 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
51. 					<HmfIcon
52. 						name="alert-help-filled"
53. 						size="xsm"
54. 						color="#000"
55. 					/>
56. 					<Text style={styles.label}>xsm (16)</Text>
57. 				</View>
58. 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
59. 					<HmfIcon
60. 						name="alert-help-filled"
61. 						size="sm"
62. 						color="#000"
63. 					/>
64. 					<Text style={styles.label}>sm (20)</Text>
65. 				</View>
66. 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
67. 					<HmfIcon
68. 						name="alert-help-filled"
69. 						size="md"

1. {/* @format */}
2. 
3. import { Meta, Canvas } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
4. 
5. <Meta title="Development/React Native Icons/Installation" />
6. 
7. # Installation:
8. 
9. This is a private repository. This means you will need proper permissions in order to use this package inside of your application.
10. 
11. #### What do you need?
12. 
13. 1. Github Access Token saved into your computers environment variables
14. 2. A .npmrc file in the root of your project to point the package installation to the github homefield repo
15. 3. GITHUB_ACTIONS_PACKAGES_PAT added to github workflow
16. 
17. #### Github Access Token
18. 
19. **For this next step. Please use the classic github Access tokens during creation.**
20. 
21. 1.  Since this is a private internal repository in order to be able to install this package you must first [ACCESS TOKEN](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/creating-a-personal-access-token) generated on your organization GitHub account.
22. 
23. [SHORTCUT TO YOUR ACCESS TOKENS](https://github.com/settings/tokens)
24. 
25. After the token is created you will want to give it access to the organization. This can be done by clicking "Configure SSO" on the token and granting authorization for dsg-tech
26. 
27. Once you have an access token with the necessary access be sure to store that token somewhere as you'll need to remember it. I would recommend storing it in our corporate LastPass!
28. 
29. 1A. Adding the Access Token to your computers environment variables
30. 
31. Mac:
32. 
33. ```bash
34. nano ~/.zshrc
35. ```
36. 
37. paste in this line
38. 
39. ```bash
40. export COMMON_ACTIONS_PACKAGES_PAT=paste_your_token_here
41. ```
42. 
43. Windows:
44. 
45. I'd recommend following this guide: Windows 10 Environment Variables
46. 
47. The important note on the above is the environment variable name must match how its referenced in the projects .npmrc so I recommend a consistency for you and your team.
48. 
49. #### NPMRC File
50. 
51. 2.  create an .npmrc at the root of your project (or the folder location your package.json exists). The .npmrc file should contain the following:
52. 
53. ```ts
54. @dsg-tech:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
55. 
56. //npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${COMMON_ACTIONS_PACKAGES_PAT}
57. ```
58. 
59. This will initialize your project that when you attempt to install the package it will also check Github Packages and look in the @dsg-tech org. However, currently you don't have your access token stored somewhere it can be accessed.
60. 
61. #### Install
62. 
63. 4. Install with
64. 
65. ```ts
66. npm install @dsg-tech/homefield-react-native-icons
67. ```
68.

 import { Meta, Canvas } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
 
 <Meta title="Development/React Native Icons/Installation" />
 
 # Installation:
 
 This is a private repository. This means you will need proper permissions in order to use this package inside of your application.
 
 #### What do you need?
 
 1. Github Access Token saved into your computers environment variables
 2. A .npmrc file in the root of your project to point the package installation to the github homefield repo
 3. GITHUB_ACTIONS_PACKAGES_PAT added to github workflow
 
 #### Github Access Token
 
 **For this next step. Please use the classic github Access tokens during creation.**
 
 1.  Since this is a private internal repository in order to be able to install this package you must first [ACCESS TOKEN](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/creating-a-personal-access-token) generated on your organization GitHub account.
 
 [SHORTCUT TO YOUR ACCESS TOKENS](https://github.com/settings/tokens)
 
 After the token is created you will want to give it access to the organization. This can be done by clicking "Configure SSO" on the token and granting authorization for dsg-tech
 
 Once you have an access token with the necessary access be sure to store that token somewhere as you'll need to remember it. I would recommend storing it in our corporate LastPass!
 
 1A. Adding the Access Token to your computers environment variables
 
 Mac:
 
 ```bash
 nano ~/.zshrc
 ```
 
 paste in this line
 
 ```bash
 export COMMON_ACTIONS_PACKAGES_PAT=paste_your_token_here
 ```
 
 Windows:
 
 I'd recommend following this guide: Windows 10 Environment Variables
 
 The important note on the above is the environment variable name must match how its referenced in the projects .npmrc so I recommend a consistency for you and your team.
 
 #### NPMRC File
 
 2.  create an .npmrc at the root of your project (or the folder location your package.json exists). The .npmrc file should contain the following:
 
 ```ts
 @dsg-tech:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
 
 //npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${COMMON_ACTIONS_PACKAGES_PAT}
 ```
 
 This will initialize your project that when you attempt to install the package it will also check Github Packages and look in the @dsg-tech org. However, currently you don't have your access token stored somewhere it can be accessed.
 
 #### Install
 
 4. Install with
 
 ```ts
 npm install @dsg-tech/homefield-react-native-icons
 ```

5. <Meta title="Development/Icons/Add Icons to the Library" />
6. 
7. # Contribution:
8. 
9. ## Approval Process:
10. 
11. If you are looking to add icon that does not currently exist in the icon library, please go here and follow the steps to present your icon proposal for consideration.
12. [How to Contribute to Homefield](https://dcsgcloud.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Homefield/pages/160566733/Learn+How+to+Contribute+to+Homefield)
13. 
14. ## Adding The New Icons:
15. 
16. After the icon has been approved by the Homefield team in the bi-weekly presentation meeting, you can add the icon to the icon library.
17. 
18. - The Homefield team will add a ticket for you on their board to work against, something like `HMF-123`
19. - Make sure the icon is a .svg
20. - the ideal default size we set the SVGs to is 24px by 24px
21. - Add the svg inside this folder `libs/icons`.
22.   - For branding icons (logos, payment cards, or multi color icons) add the icon into the `multi-color` directory.
23.   - For most icons, add the icon into the `single-color` directory.
24. - Once the icon is added to icons folder, commit them to your branch and open a PR. The builds are now completed via GitHub Actions.
25. 
26. - Open a pull request following [this guide](https://dcsgcloud.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Homefield/pages/568197177/Pull+Request+Reviews) and assign it to the Homefield team for review.
27.

 <Meta title="Development/Icons/Add Icons to the Library" />
 
 # Contribution:
 
 ## Approval Process:
 
 If you are looking to add icon that does not currently exist in the icon library, please go here and follow the steps to present your icon proposal for consideration.
 [How to Contribute to Homefield](https://dcsgcloud.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Homefield/pages/160566733/Learn+How+to+Contribute+to+Homefield)
 
 ## Adding The New Icons:
 
 After the icon has been approved by the Homefield team in the bi-weekly presentation meeting, you can add the icon to the icon library.
 
 - The Homefield team will add a ticket for you on their board to work against, something like `HMF-123`
 - Make sure the icon is a .svg
 - the ideal default size we set the SVGs to is 24px by 24px
 - Add the svg inside this folder `libs/icons`.
   - For branding icons (logos, payment cards, or multi color icons) add the icon into the `multi-color` directory.
   - For most icons, add the icon into the `single-color` directory.
 - Once the icon is added to icons folder, commit them to your branch and open a PR. The builds are now completed via GitHub Actions.
 
 - Open a pull request following [this guide](https://dcsgcloud.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Homefield/pages/568197177/Pull+Request+Reviews) and assign it to the Homefield team for review.

data.content:
## React Native icon registry workflow (current)

### 1) Where registration/registry/loading is implemented (RN libs + app)
- **`/Users/DKS0700123/Projects/homefield/libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts`**
  - Exposes singleton registry + manual API:
  - `export const iconRegistry = new IconRegistryService<IconComponent>();`
  - `export const registerIcons = (icons) => iconRegistry.registerIcons(icons);`
- **`/Users/DKS0700123/Projects/homefield/libs/output/hmf-utils/src/lib/helpers/iconRegistry/iconRegistry.ts`**
  - Shared generic registry implementation (`Map<string, {base, sizes}>`), `getIconBySize`, `clear`, etc.
- **`/Users/DKS0700123/Projects/homefield/libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx`**
  - Dynamic renderer by `name` + size alias -> numeric -> `iconRegistry.getIconBySize(name, numericSize)`.
  - If unregistered: warns + returns `null`.
- **`/Users/DKS0700123/Projects/homefield/tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`**
  - Generates RN icon components + barrel exports from `libs/icons/**`.
  - Writes `assets/index.ts` + `assets-multi/index.ts`; does **not** generate a runtime name→component registry helper.

### 2) Where icons are consumed + how devs add a new icon
- RN app (`apps/react-native`) is Storybook entry only (`App.tsx` loads `.storybook`).
- Consumption patterns in RN icon lib:
  - **Direct component use (no registry):** `IconsDirect.stories.tsx` imports `* as AllIcons`.
  - **Dynamic use (`HmfIcon`) requires registration:** `IconsRegistration.stories.tsx` calls:
    ```ts
    registerIcons([['alert-help-filled', AlertHelpFilled, {12: AlertHelpFilled12, ...}]])
    ```
- Docs for adding icons:
  - **`hmf-react-native-icons-contribution.docs.mdx`** says add SVG to `libs/icons`, run generation command.
  - Build pipeline confirms generation is via Nx prebuild:
    - **`libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/project.json`** -> `prebuild` runs `node ./tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`.

### 3) Manual registration APIs/mappings causing client-side steps
- Manual API: `registerIcons(...)` in `IconRegistry.ts`.
- Manual runtime map entries required for dynamic `HmfIcon` names, including per-size maps (`{12: Comp12, 16: Comp16, ...}`).
- No RN equivalent of generated `register-all.ts` / name manifest (web has one at `hmf-web-icons/src/lib/icon-library/register-all.ts`).
- RN `HmfIcon` accepts `name: string` (no generated union type), so registration/name mismatch is runtime-only.

### 4) Tests + coverage gaps
- Existing RN tests:
  - **`/libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx`**
  - Covers aliases, basic registry ops, unregistered behavior, size resolution.
- Gaps:
  - No tests for generated outputs/integrity of `generate-rn-icons.js`.
  - No tests for bulk/auto-registration workflow (because none exists).
  - No tests for multi-color dynamic registration flow.
  - “fallback” test is weak/misleading: it says fallback case but uses `xxlg` (48), which actually has a registered variant.

### 5) Key files (what each does)
- `.../IconRegistry.ts` — registry singleton + `registerIcons`.
- `.../HmfIcon.tsx` — dynamic lookup/render; fails null if not registered.
- `.../hmf-utils/.../iconRegistry.ts` — core generic registry logic.
- `.../assets/index.ts` — generated exports of all RN icon components.
- `.../tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` — icon component generation pipeline.
- `.../IconsRegistration.stories.tsx` — canonical manual registration example.
- `.../IconsDirect.stories.tsx` — direct import usage path (bypasses registry).
- `.../Icons.test.tsx` — only RN icon registry test file.

### 6) 2–3 concrete DX improvements (Nx/TS-friendly)
1. **Generate RN `registerAllIcons()` + manifest during prebuild**
   - Extend `generate-rn-icons.js` to emit `icon-manifest.generated.ts` + `register-all.ts` (like web).
   - Optional: `registerIconsFromManifest(['alert-help-filled', ...])`.

2. **Generate typed icon name union for RN**
   - Emit `icon-names.generated.ts` (`ALL_ICON_NAMES`, `type HomefieldIconName`).
   - Change `HmfIconProps.name` from `string` to `HomefieldIconName` for compile-time safety.

3. **Auto-build registration map from `assets` exports**
   - Generate helper to parse `AlertHelpFilled32` naming into base + size and auto-register at startup.
   - Keeps tree-shaking option: provide both `registerAllIcons()` and selective `registerIconsByName([...])`.
data.reasoningText: **Summarizing icon package inconsistencies and test gaps**

# React Native Icon Registry DX Plan

## Problem statement
The current React Native dynamic icon flow is not DX-friendly because developers must manually call `registerIcons(...)` and maintain base-name/size mappings on the client side. This creates repetitive setup, runtime-only mistakes, and weak discoverability when adding new icons.

## Current state summary
- Runtime registration entrypoint: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts`
- Dynamic icon rendering/lookup: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx`
- Generic registry logic: `libs/output/hmf-utils/src/lib/helpers/iconRegistry/iconRegistry.ts`
- Icon generation script: `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`
- Generated icon exports currently exist (`assets/index.ts`, `assets-multi/index.ts`) but no generated name->size registry helper.
- Current test coverage is concentrated in `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx`; no dedicated tests for generated registration artifacts.

## Proposed approach
Introduce **generated registration artifacts** as part of the existing RN icon generation pipeline, while preserving backward compatibility with the existing manual API:

1. Extend `generate-rn-icons.js` to emit:
   - `icon-manifest.generated.ts` (base icon name -> component refs by size)
   - `register-all.generated.ts` (`registerAllIcons()` and optional selective registration helper)

# React Native Icon Registry DX Plan

## Problem statement
The current React Native dynamic icon flow is not DX-friendly because developers must manually call `registerIcons(...)` and maintain base-name/size mappings on the client side. This creates repetitive setup, runtime-only mistakes, and weak discoverability when adding new icons.

## Current state summary
- Runtime registration entrypoint: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts`
- Dynamic icon rendering/lookup: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx`
- Generic registry logic: `libs/output/hmf-utils/src/lib/helpers/iconRegistry/iconRegistry.ts`
- Icon generation script: `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`
- Generated icon exports currently exist (`assets/index.ts`, `assets-multi/index.ts`) but no generated name->size registry helper.
- Current test coverage is concentrated in `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx`; no dedicated tests for generated registration artifacts.

## Proposed approach
Introduce **generated registration artifacts** as part of the existing RN icon generation pipeline, while preserving backward compatibility with the existing manual API:

1. Extend `generate-rn-icons.js` to emit:
   - `icon-manifest.generated.ts` (base icon name -> component refs by size)
   - `register-all.generated.ts` (`registerAllIcons()` and optional selective registration helper)
   - `icon-names.generated.ts` (`ALL_ICON_NAMES` + `HomefieldIconName` union)
2. Export generated helpers from the RN icon package public API.
3. Update `HmfIcon` typing to use generated icon-name type where feasible (or a compatible transitional type if needed).
4. Keep `registerIcons(...)` available for non-breaking migration.
5. Add tests for both runtime behavior and generation outputs.

## Implementation todos
1. **Define registration contract**
   - Finalize shape for generated manifest and helper signatures.
   - Decide default DX behavior (explicit `registerAllIcons()` call vs auto-register on import).

2. **Extend generator**
   - Modify `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` to produce manifest + registration helper + name union outputs.
   - Ensure generated files are deterministic and stable for CI.

3. **Wire runtime/public API**
   - Export generated helpers from RN icon library entrypoints.
   - Integrate with existing `iconRegistry` utilities without breaking current consumers.

4. **Strengthen type safety**
   - Introduce generated icon-name type and thread it into `HmfIcon` props.
   - Preserve compatibility for any legacy string-based usages if needed.

5. **Add test coverage**
   - Unit tests for generated registration helper behavior (all icons + selective icons).
   - Unit tests for `HmfIcon` resolution using generated registration flow.
   - Generator-level test(s) verifying expected generated outputs/contract.

6. **Update developer docs**
   - Update RN icon contribution docs to replace/manual registration steps with generated workflow.
   - Add migration notes and usage examples.

## Test plan
- Run existing RN icon tests and affected workspace tests before and after changes.
- Add/execute new tests for:
  - generated manifest/register helper contract,
  - runtime registry integration,
  - typed icon-name behavior.
- Validate generation via existing Nx prebuild path for `hmf-react-native-icons`.

## Risks and mitigations
- **Bundle-size risk** if everything auto-registers by default:
  - Mitigate by providing both `registerAllIcons()` and selective registration APIs.
- **Backward-compatibility risk**:
  - Keep manual `registerIcons(...)` API and layer new helpers on top.
- **Generator drift risk**:
  - Add deterministic output tests and keep generated file shape explicit.

## Open question for product/consumer behavior
Should the library optimize for zero-setup DX by default (auto-register all icons), or keep registration explicit (recommended helper call) to preserve tighter bundle control?
EOF

cat > /Users/DKS0700123/.copilot/session-state/e1db69e0-275a-44b1-9c28-f0243f6db412/plan.md <<'EOF'
# React Native Icon Registry DX Plan

## Problem statement
The current React Native dynamic icon flow is DX-hostile because engineers must manually call `registerIcons(...)` and build name/size maps on the client. The target outcome is a **zero-manual-registration** experience where `HmfIcon` works after normal package import.

## Current state summary
- Runtime registration entrypoint: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts`
- Dynamic icon rendering/lookup: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx`
- Shared registry implementation: `libs/output/hmf-utils/src/lib/helpers/iconRegistry/iconRegistry.ts`
- Icon generation pipeline: `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`
- Generated exports exist (`assets/index.ts`, `assets-multi/index.ts`) but no generated runtime bootstrap to auto-register icons.
- Existing tests mainly in `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx`; there is no dedicated coverage proving a no-registration-required flow.

## Proposed approach
Implement generated, automatic icon registration as the default path:

1. Extend `generate-rn-icons.js` to generate:
   - `icon-manifest.generated.ts`: canonical base-name -> size component map
   - `auto-register.generated.ts`: bootstrap that registers every generated icon at module init
   - `icon-names.generated.ts`: `ALL_ICON_NAMES` + `HomefieldIconName`
2. Import/execute the generated auto-register bootstrap from the RN icon library entrypoint so consumers do not manually register.
3. Keep legacy `registerIcons(...)` available only for backward compatibility (not required for normal usage).
4. Type `HmfIcon` name with generated union to improve discoverability and compile-time safety.
5. Add tests that lock in zero-setup behavior and generator correctness.

## Implementation todos
1. **Define auto-registration contract**
   - Specify generated manifest shape and bootstrap execution order.
   - Guarantee idempotent registration and deterministic generated output.

2. **Extend RN icon generator**
   - Update `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` to emit manifest/bootstrap/name-union files.
   - Ensure generated files are stable and CI-safe.

3. **Wire zero-setup runtime path**
   - Hook generated bootstrap into package initialization.
   - Ensure `HmfIcon` resolves icons by name with no client registration code.

4. **Strengthen types**
   - Apply generated `HomefieldIconName` to `HmfIcon` and exposed APIs where applicable.

5. **Add comprehensive tests**
   - Runtime tests: render dynamic icon without any registration call.
   - Registry tests: idempotence, size resolution, and missing-name behavior.
   - Generator tests: expected generated contract and bootstrap content.

6. **Update docs/migration guidance**
   - Replace manual registration guidance with zero-setup usage.
   - Add migration notes from legacy manual registration pattern.

## Test plan
- Baseline: run existing affected tests before code changes.
- After implementation:
  - run RN icon library tests,
  - run new runtime + generator tests,
  - run affected Nx targets for icon generation/prebuild.
- Explicitly verify: `HmfIcon` works from clean consumer code path without calling `registerIcons(...)`.

## Risks and mitigations
- **Startup/bundle overhead from auto-registering all icons**
  - Measure via existing build/test workflow; if needed, optimize generated bootstrap internals without requiring consumer-side manual mapping.
- **Behavior regressions in existing consumers**
  - Preserve `registerIcons(...)` compatibility and add regression tests around existing API.
- **Generator drift**
  - Add deterministic contract assertions for generated artifacts.
EOF

   - Generator tests: expected generated contract and bootstrap content.

6. **Update docs/migration guidance**
   - Replace manual registration guidance with zero-setup usage.
   - Add migration notes from legacy manual registration pattern.

## Test plan
- Baseline: run existing affected tests before code changes.
- After implementation:
  - run RN icon library tests,
  - run new runtime + generator tests,
  - run affected Nx targets for icon generation/prebuild.
- Explicitly verify: `HmfIcon` works from clean consumer code path without calling `registerIcons(...)`.

## Risks and mitigations
- **Startup/bundle overhead from auto-registering all icons**
  - Measure via existing build/test workflow; if needed, optimize generated bootstrap internals without requiring consumer-side manual mapping.
- **Behavior regressions in existing consumers**
  - Preserve `registerIcons(...)` compatibility and add regression tests around existing API.
- **Generator drift**
  - Add deterministic contract assertions for generated artifacts.
EOF

1. # React Native Icon Registry DX Plan
2. 
3. ## Problem statement
4. The current React Native dynamic icon flow is DX-hostile because engineers must manually call `registerIcons(...)` and build name/size maps on the client. The target outcome is a **zero-manual-registration** experience where `HmfIcon` works after normal package import.
5. 
6. ## Current state summary
7. - Runtime registration entrypoint: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts`
8. - Dynamic icon rendering/lookup: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx`
9. - Shared registry implementation: `libs/output/hmf-utils/src/lib/helpers/iconRegistry/iconRegistry.ts`
10. - Icon generation pipeline: `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`
11. - Generated exports exist (`assets/index.ts`, `assets-multi/index.ts`) but no generated runtime bootstrap to auto-register icons.
12. - Existing tests mainly in `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx`; there is no dedicated coverage proving a no-registration-required flow.
13. 
14. ## Proposed approach
15. Implement generated, automatic icon registration as the default path:
16. 
17. 1. Extend `generate-rn-icons.js` to generate:
18.    - `icon-manifest.generated.ts`: canonical base-name -> size component map
19.    - `auto-register.generated.ts`: bootstrap that registers every generated icon at module init
20.    - `icon-names.generated.ts`: `ALL_ICON_NAMES` + `HomefieldIconName`
21. 2. Import/execute the generated auto-register bootstrap from the RN icon library entrypoint so consumers do not manually register.
22. 3. Keep legacy `registerIcons(...)` available only for backward compatibility (not required for normal usage).
23. 4. Type `HmfIcon` name with generated union to improve discoverability and compile-time safety.
24. 5. Add tests that lock in zero-setup behavior and generator correctness.
25. 
26. ## Implementation todos
27. 1. **Define auto-registration contract**
28.    - Specify generated manifest shape and bootstrap execution order.
29.    - Guarantee idempotent registration and deterministic generated output.
30. 
31. 2. **Extend RN icon generator**
32.    - Update `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` to emit manifest/bootstrap/name-union files.
33.    - Ensure generated files are stable and CI-safe.
34. 
35. 3. **Wire zero-setup runtime path**
36.    - Hook generated bootstrap into package initialization.
37.    - Ensure `HmfIcon` resolves icons by name with no client registration code.
38. 
39. 4. **Strengthen types**
40.    - Apply generated `HomefieldIconName` to `HmfIcon` and exposed APIs where applicable.
41. 
42. 5. **Add comprehensive tests**
43.    - Runtime tests: render dynamic icon without any registration call.
44.    - Registry tests: idempotence, size resolution, and missing-name behavior.
45.    - Generator tests: expected generated contract and bootstrap content.
46. 
47. 6. **Update docs/migration guidance**
48.    - Replace manual registration guidance with zero-setup usage.
49.    - Add migration notes from legacy manual registration pattern.
50. 
51. ## Test plan
52. - Baseline: run existing affected tests before code changes.
53. - After implementation:
54.   - run RN icon library tests,
55.   - run new runtime + generator tests,
56.   - run affected Nx targets for icon generation/prebuild.
57. - Explicitly verify: `HmfIcon` works from clean consumer code path without calling `registerIcons(...)`.
58. 
59. ## Risks and mitigations
60. - **Startup/bundle overhead from auto-registering all icons**
61.   - Measure via existing build/test workflow; if needed, optimize generated bootstrap internals without requiring consumer-side manual mapping.
62. - **Behavior regressions in existing consumers**
63.   - Preserve `registerIcons(...)` compatibility and add regression tests around existing API.
64. - **Generator drift**
65.   - Add deterministic contract assertions for generated artifacts.
66.

@@ -1,66 +1,66 @@
 # React Native Icon Registry DX Plan
 
 ## Problem statement
 The current React Native dynamic icon flow is DX-hostile because engineers must manually call `registerIcons(...)` and build name/size maps on the client. The target outcome is a **zero-manual-registration** experience where `HmfIcon` works after normal package import.
 
 ## Current state summary
 - Runtime registration entrypoint: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts`
 - Dynamic icon rendering/lookup: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx`
 - Shared registry implementation: `libs/output/hmf-utils/src/lib/helpers/iconRegistry/iconRegistry.ts`
 - Icon generation pipeline: `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`
 - Generated exports exist (`assets/index.ts`, `assets-multi/index.ts`) but no generated runtime bootstrap to auto-register icons.
 - Existing tests mainly in `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx`; there is no dedicated coverage proving a no-registration-required flow.
 
 ## Proposed approach
 Implement generated, automatic icon registration as the default path:
 
 1. Extend `generate-rn-icons.js` to generate:
    - `icon-manifest.generated.ts`: canonical base-name -> size component map
    - `auto-register.generated.ts`: bootstrap that registers every generated icon at module init
    - `icon-names.generated.ts`: `ALL_ICON_NAMES` + `HomefieldIconName`
 2. Import/execute the generated auto-register bootstrap from the RN icon library entrypoint so consumers do not manually register.
 3. Keep legacy `registerIcons(...)` available only for backward compatibility (not required for normal usage).
 4. Type `HmfIcon` name with generated union to improve discoverability and compile-time safety.
 5. Add tests that lock in zero-setup behavior and generator correctness.
 
 ## Implementation todos
 1. **Define auto-registration contract**
    - Specify generated manifest shape and bootstrap execution order.
    - Guarantee idempotent registration and deterministic generated output.
 
 2. **Extend RN icon generator**
    - Update `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` to emit manifest/bootstrap/name-union files.
    - Ensure generated files are stable and CI-safe.
 
 3. **Wire zero-setup runtime path**
    - Hook generated bootstrap into package initialization.
    - Ensure `HmfIcon` resolves icons by name with no client registration code.
 
 4. **Strengthen types**
    - Apply generated `HomefieldIconName` to `HmfIcon` and exposed APIs where applicable.
 
 5. **Add comprehensive tests**
    - Runtime tests: render dynamic icon without any registration call.
    - Registry tests: idempotence, size resolution, and missing-name behavior.
    - Generator tests: expected generated contract and bootstrap content.
 
 6. **Update docs/migration guidance**
    - Replace manual registration guidance with zero-setup usage.
    - Add migration notes from legacy manual registration pattern.
 
 ## Test plan
 - Baseline: run existing affected tests before code changes.
 - After implementation:
   - run RN icon library tests,
   - run new runtime + generator tests,
   - run affected Nx targets for icon generation/prebuild.
 - Explicitly verify: `HmfIcon` works from clean consumer code path without calling `registerIcons(...)`.
 
 ## Risks and mitigations
 - **Startup/bundle overhead from auto-registering all icons**
   - Measure via existing build/test workflow; if needed, optimize generated bootstrap internals without requiring consumer-side manual mapping.
 - **Behavior regressions in existing consumers**
   - Preserve `registerIcons(...)` compatibility and add regression tests around existing API.
 - **Generator drift**
   - Add deterministic contract assertions for generated artifacts.

1. /** @format */
2. 
3. /**
4.  * Homefield Web Icons
5.  *
6.  * Framework-agnostic icon library for the Homefield Design System.
7.  * Works with Angular, React, Vue, or vanilla JavaScript.
8.  *
9.  * @example
10.  * ```typescript
11.  * // Option 1: Import specific icons (recommended for production)
12.  * import { registerIcons, homefieldIconArrowLeft } from '@dsg-tech/homefield-web-icons';
13.  *
14.  * registerIcons([homefieldIconArrowLeft]);
15.  *
16.  * // Option 2: Import all icons (for development/demos)
17.  * import { registerAllIcons } from '@dsg-tech/homefield-web-icons/register-all';
18.  *
19.  * registerAllIcons();
20.  *
21.  * // 3. Use in HTML
22.  * // <hmf-icon name="arrow-left" size="md"></hmf-icon>
23.  * ```
24.  */
25. 
26. // Export custom elements
27. export { HmfIcon } from './components/hmf-icon';
28. export { HmfIconGallery } from './lib/icon-gallery.component';
29. 
30. // Export registry
31. export { IconRegistry, getIconRegistry, registerIcons } from './lib/icon-library/icon-registry';
32. 
33. // Export icon names and types
34. export { ALL_ICON_NAMES, ICON_SIZE_VARIANTS } from './lib/icon-library/icon-names.generated';
35. 
36. export type { HomefieldIconName } from './lib/icon-library/icon-names.generated';
37. 
38. // Re-export all icon assets for convenience
39. export * from './lib/icon-library/assets';
40. export * from './lib/icon-library/assets-multi';
41. 
42. // Export constants
43. export * from './lib/constants';
44. 
45. // Export types
46. export type { IconRegistration, IconModule, IconSize, IconSizeAlias } from './lib/types/icon-types';
47. 
48. // Export utilities
49. export { resolveSizeToPixels } from './lib/utils/size-resolver';
50. export { parseSVG, sanitizeSVG } from './lib/utils/svg-parser';
51.

arguments.prompt:
Implement a zero-manual-registration DX improvement for React Native icons in /Users/DKS0700123/Projects/homefield.

Context:
- Current dynamic icon component (`HmfIcon`) requires consumers to manually call `registerIcons(...)`.
- We need `HmfIcon` to work out-of-the-box without client-side manual icon registration.
- Keep backward compatibility: existing `registerIcons` API should still work.
- Add tests that prove zero-setup behavior.

Relevant files:
- libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts
- libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx
- libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx
- libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/index.ts
- libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconsDirect.stories.tsx
- libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/documentation/hmf-react-native-icons-use.docs.mdx
- libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/documentation/hmf-react-native-icons-contribution.docs.mdx

Implement this approach:
1) Add a new helper module under `icon-library` (e.g., `auto-register.ts`) that:
   - Imports all icon exports from `./assets`.
   - Builds registrations automatically by parsing export names:
     - suffix numeric variants 12/16/20/24/32/40/48 treated as size variants
     - base icon name converted from PascalCase to kebab-case
   - Exposes:
     - `registerAllIcons()`
     - `ensureIconsRegistered()` (idempotent; if registry was cleared in tests, should register again)
     - `ALL_ICON_NAMES`
     - `HomefieldIconName` type (best effort from ALL_ICON_NAMES)
     - test-only reset helper (`__resetAutoRegistrationForTests`) for deterministic tests
2) Update `HmfIcon.tsx`:
   - Call `ensureIconsRegistered()` before lookup.
   - Update docs/comments to reflect auto-registration default.
   - Use `HomefieldIconName` type for `name` prop.
3) Update `src/index.ts` to export `registerAllIcons`, `ALL_ICON_NAMES`, and `HomefieldIconName`.
4) Update tests in `Icons.test.tsx`:
   - Add tests proving no manual registration is needed for known icon names.
   - Add test for registerAllIcons idempotence.
   - Keep legacy manual registry tests intact.
   - Ensure tests reset auto-registration state when clearing registry.
5) Update docs/stories so examples do not require manual registration for HmfIcon usage.

Constraints:
- Make precise edits only in relevant files.
- Follow existing formatting/style.
- If running tests is possible, run `nx test hmf-react-native-icons`; if not possible due permission, note that in output.

Return:
- concise summary of changed files
- any test command output or limitation
- key behavior changes

 /** @format */
 
 import React from 'react';
 import { View, Text, ScrollView, StyleSheet } from 'react-native';
 import { Meta, StoryObj } from '@storybook/react';
 import {
 	HmfIcon,
 	registerIcons,
 	AlertHelpFilled,
 	AlertHelpFilled12,
 	AlertHelpFilled16,
 	AlertHelpFilled20,
 	AlertHelpFilled32,
 	AlertHelpFilled40,
 	AlertHelpFilled48
 } from '../../..';
 
 // Register alert-help-filled with its per-size SVG variants
 registerIcons([
 	[
 		'alert-help-filled',
 		AlertHelpFilled,
 		{
 			12: AlertHelpFilled12,
 			16: AlertHelpFilled16,
 			20: AlertHelpFilled20,
 			32: AlertHelpFilled32,
 			40: AlertHelpFilled40,
 			48: AlertHelpFilled48
 		}
 	]
 ]);
 
 const RegistrationShowcase = () => (
 	<ScrollView style={styles.container}>
 		<View style={styles.section}>
 			<Text style={styles.sectionTitle}>Size Variant Resolution</Text>
 			<Text style={{ fontSize: 14, color: '#666', marginBottom: 12 }}>
 				Each size resolves to a dedicated SVG optimized for that pixel size.
 			</Text>
 			<View style={styles.row}>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size="xxsm"
 						color="#000"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>xxsm (12)</Text>
 				</View>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size="xsm"
 						color="#000"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>xsm (16)</Text>
 				</View>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size="sm"
 						color="#000"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>sm (20)</Text>
 				</View>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size="md"
 						color="#000"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>md (24)</Text>
 				</View>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size="lg"
 						color="#000"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>lg (32)</Text>
 				</View>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size="xlg"
 						color="#000"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>xlg (40)</Text>
 				</View>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size="xxlg"
 						color="#000"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>xxlg (48)</Text>
 				</View>
 			</View>
 		</View>
 
 		<View style={styles.section}>
 			<Text style={styles.sectionTitle}>Numeric Sizes</Text>
 			<View style={styles.row}>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size={16}
 						color="#333"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>16px</Text>
 				</View>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>

 						size={24}
 						color="#333"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>24px</Text>
 				</View>
 				<View style={styles.iconBox}>
 					<HmfIcon
 						name="alert-help-filled"
 						size={48}
 						color="#333"
 					/>
 					<Text style={styles.label}>48px</Text>
 				</View>
 			</View>
 		</View>
 
 		<View style={styles.section}>

 );
 
 const meta = {

 } satisfies Meta<typeof RegistrationShowcase>;
 
 export default meta;

 ## Risks and mitigations
 - **Startup/bundle overhead from auto-registering all icons**
   - Measure via existing build/test workflow; if needed, optimize generated bootstrap internals without requiring consumer-side manual mapping.
 - **Behavior regressions in existing consumers**
   - Preserve `registerIcons(...)` compatibility and add regression tests around existing API.
 - **Generator drift**
   - Add deterministic contract assertions for generated artifacts.
+
+## Progress update
+- Repository write access and Node/Nx execution are currently blocked in this environment (`Permission denied and could not request permission from user`).
+- A ready-to-apply implementation patch has been prepared at:
+  - `/Users/DKS0700123/.copilot/session-state/e1db69e0-275a-44b1-9c28-f0243f6db412/files/rn-icon-dx.patch`
+- The patch includes:
+  - automatic icon registration module for RN (`auto-register.ts`),
+  - `HmfIcon` integration + typed icon-name surface,
+  - public API export updates,
+  - tests for zero-setup behavior and idempotent registration,
+  - docs/story updates removing manual `registerIcons(...)` setup.
+
+## Next execution step once permissions are available
+1. Apply patch to repository.
+2. Run validation targets:
+   - `nx test hmf-react-native-icons`
+   - `nx lint hmf-react-native-icons`
+   - `nx build hmf-react-native-icons`
+3. Address any test/lint issues and finalize.
*** End Patch

+{/* @format */}
+
+import { Meta, Canvas } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
+
+<Meta title="Development/React Native Icons/Use" />
+
+# How To Use A Homefield Icon
+
+Homefield React Native icons can be used either as direct component imports or with the dynamic `HmfIcon` renderer.
+All generated icons are auto-registered for `HmfIcon`, so no manual registry setup is required.
+
+## Direct component import
+
+```typescript
+import { AlertAnnouncement } from '@dsg-tech/homefield-react-native-icons';
+```
+
+### Basic example
+
+```tsx
+import React from 'react';
+import { AlertAnnouncement } from '@dsg-tech/homefield-react-native-icons';
+
+const App = () => {
+	return <AlertAnnouncement />;
+};
+```
+
+### Override props example
+
+```tsx
+import React from 'react';
+import { AlertAnnouncement } from '@dsg-tech/homefield-react-native-icons';
+
+const App = () => {
+	// You can use any valid SVG element prop as an override
+	return (
+		<AlertAnnouncement
+			width={50}
+			height={50}
+			fill="#ffffff"
+		/>
+	);
+};
+```
+
+## Dynamic usage with `HmfIcon` (auto-registered)
+
+```tsx
+import React from 'react';
+import { HmfIcon } from '@dsg-tech/homefield-react-native-icons';
+
+const App = () => {
+	return (
+		<HmfIcon
+			name="alert-help-filled"
+			size="lg"
+			color="#000000"
+		/>
+	);
+};
+```
+

+{/* @format */}
+
+import { Meta, Canvas } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
+
+<Meta title="Development/React Native Icons/Contribution" />
+
+# Contribution:
+
+Contributing to the React Native icon library is straightforward.
+
+1. Add your approved SVG to `libs/icons/single-color` or `libs/icons/multi-color`.
+
+2. Regenerate the React Native icon components:
+
+```bash
+npx nx run hmf-react-native-icons:prebuild
+```
+

Attempt to directly implement and validate the zero-manual-registration RN icon DX fix in /Users/DKS0700123/Projects/homefield.

Required code changes:
1) Add auto registration helper at libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/auto-register.ts:
   - import * as iconAssets from './assets'
   - parse PascalCase component exports into kebab-case icon names
   - treat numeric suffixes 12/16/20/24/32/40/48 as size variants
   - export registerAllIcons(), ensureIconsRegistered() (idempotent and re-registers after registry clear), ALL_ICON_NAMES, HomefieldIconName, __resetAutoRegistrationForTests()
2) Update HmfIcon.tsx to call ensureIconsRegistered() before lookup and type name prop as HomefieldIconName.
3) Update src/index.ts exports to include registerAllIcons, ALL_ICON_NAMES, HomefieldIconName.
4) Update Icons.test.tsx:
   - add auto-registration tests (known icon names and idempotent registerAllIcons)
   - ensure HmfIcon works without manual registerIcons call
   - keep existing registry tests, reset auto-registration state in beforeEach where clearing registry
5) Update IconsRegistration.stories.tsx to remove manual registerIcons setup import/call.
6) Update docs:
   - hmf-react-native-icons-use.docs.mdx to document auto-registration for HmfIcon
   - hmf-react-native-icons-contribution.docs.mdx to use nx prebuild command and no manual register mapping

Then run:
- nx test hmf-react-native-icons
- nx lint hmf-react-native-icons
- nx build hmf-react-native-icons

arguments.description: Compare artifact files to repo files
arguments.command:
set -euo pipefail
ROOT=/Users/DKS0700123/.copilot/session-state/e1db69e0-275a-44b1-9c28-f0243f6db412/files/rn-icon-dx-proposed
REPO=/Users/DKS0700123/Projects/homefield
files=(
  libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/auto-register.ts
  libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx
  libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx
  libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconsRegistration.stories.tsx
  libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/documentation/hmf-react-native-icons-use.docs.mdx
  libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/documentation/hmf-react-native-icons-contribution.docs.mdx
  libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/index.ts
)
for f in "${files[@]}"; do
  if [ -f "$REPO/$f" ]; then
    if cmp -s "$ROOT/$f" "$REPO/$f"; then
      echo "UNCHANGED $f"
    else
      echo "DIFF $f"
    fi
  else
    echo "NEW $f"
  fi
done

5. <Meta title="Development/React Native Icons/Contribution" />
6. 
7. # Contribution:
8. 
9. Contributing to the React Native icon library is straightforward.
10. 
11. 1. Add your approved SVG to `libs/icons/single-color` or `libs/icons/multi-color`.
12. 
13. 2. Regenerate the React Native icon components:
14. 
15. ```bash
16. npx nx run hmf-react-native-icons:prebuild
17. ```
18. 
19. This runs `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` and refreshes the generated icon exports.
20. No manual `registerIcons(...)` mapping is required for dynamic `HmfIcon` usage.

 import { Meta, Canvas } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
 
 <Meta title="Development/React Native Icons/Contribution" />
 
 # Contribution:
 
 Contributing to the React Native icon library is straightforward.
 
 1. Add your approved SVG to `libs/icons/single-color` or `libs/icons/multi-color`.
 
 2. Regenerate the React Native icon components:
 
 ```bash
 npx nx run hmf-react-native-icons:prebuild
 ```
 
 This runs `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` and refreshes the generated icon exports.
 No manual `registerIcons(...)` mapping is required for dynamic `HmfIcon` usage.

1. # React Native Icon Registry DX Plan
2. 
3. ## Problem statement
4. The current React Native dynamic icon flow is DX-hostile because engineers must manually call `registerIcons(...)` and build name/size maps on the client. The target outcome is a **zero-manual-registration** experience where `HmfIcon` works after normal package import.
5. 
6. ## Current state summary
7. - Runtime registration entrypoint: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts`
8. - Dynamic icon rendering/lookup: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx`
9. - Shared registry implementation: `libs/output/hmf-utils/src/lib/helpers/iconRegistry/iconRegistry.ts`
10. - Icon generation pipeline: `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`
11. - Generated exports exist (`assets/index.ts`, `assets-multi/index.ts`) but no generated runtime bootstrap to auto-register icons.
12. - Existing tests mainly in `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx`; there is no dedicated coverage proving a no-registration-required flow.
13. 
14. ## Proposed approach
15. Implement generated, automatic icon registration as the default path:
16. 
17. 1. Extend `generate-rn-icons.js` to generate:
18.    - `icon-manifest.generated.ts`: canonical base-name -> size component map
19.    - `auto-register.generated.ts`: bootstrap that registers every generated icon at module init
20.    - `icon-names.generated.ts`: `ALL_ICON_NAMES` + `HomefieldIconName`
21. 2. Import/execute the generated auto-register bootstrap from the RN icon library entrypoint so consumers do not manually register.
22. 3. Keep legacy `registerIcons(...)` available only for backward compatibility (not required for normal usage).
23. 4. Type `HmfIcon` name with generated union to improve discoverability and compile-time safety.
24. 5. Add tests that lock in zero-setup behavior and generator correctness.
25. 
26. ## Implementation todos
27. 1. **Define auto-registration contract**
28.    - Specify generated manifest shape and bootstrap execution order.
29.    - Guarantee idempotent registration and deterministic generated output.
30. 
31. 2. **Extend RN icon generator**
32.    - Update `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` to emit manifest/bootstrap/name-union files.
33.    - Ensure generated files are stable and CI-safe.
34. 
35. 3. **Wire zero-setup runtime path**
36.    - Hook generated bootstrap into package initialization.
37.    - Ensure `HmfIcon` resolves icons by name with no client registration code.
38. 
39. 4. **Strengthen types**
40.    - Apply generated `HomefieldIconName` to `HmfIcon` and exposed APIs where applicable.
41. 
42. 5. **Add comprehensive tests**
43.    - Runtime tests: render dynamic icon without any registration call.
44.    - Registry tests: idempotence, size resolution, and missing-name behavior.
45.    - Generator tests: expected generated contract and bootstrap content.
46. 
47. 6. **Update docs/migration guidance**
48.    - Replace manual registration guidance with zero-setup usage.
49.    - Add migration notes from legacy manual registration pattern.
50. 
51. ## Test plan
52. - Baseline: run existing affected tests before code changes.
53. - After implementation:
54.   - run RN icon library tests,
55.   - run new runtime + generator tests,
56.   - run affected Nx targets for icon generation/prebuild.
57. - Explicitly verify: `HmfIcon` works from clean consumer code path without calling `registerIcons(...)`.
58. 
59. ## Risks and mitigations
60. - **Startup/bundle overhead from auto-registering all icons**
61.   - Measure via existing build/test workflow; if needed, optimize generated bootstrap internals without requiring consumer-side manual mapping.
62. - **Behavior regressions in existing consumers**
63.   - Preserve `registerIcons(...)` compatibility and add regression tests around existing API.
64. - **Generator drift**
65.   - Add deterministic contract assertions for generated artifacts.
66.

 # React Native Icon Registry DX Plan
 
 ## Problem statement
 The current React Native dynamic icon flow is DX-hostile because engineers must manually call `registerIcons(...)` and build name/size maps on the client. The target outcome is a **zero-manual-registration** experience where `HmfIcon` works after normal package import.
 
 ## Current state summary
 - Runtime registration entrypoint: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/IconRegistry.ts`
 - Dynamic icon rendering/lookup: `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/HmfIcon.tsx`
 - Shared registry implementation: `libs/output/hmf-utils/src/lib/helpers/iconRegistry/iconRegistry.ts`
 - Icon generation pipeline: `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js`
 - Generated exports exist (`assets/index.ts`, `assets-multi/index.ts`) but no generated runtime bootstrap to auto-register icons.
 - Existing tests mainly in `libs/output/hmf-react-native-icons/src/lib/components/icon-library/Icons.test.tsx`; there is no dedicated coverage proving a no-registration-required flow.
 
 ## Proposed approach
 Implement generated, automatic icon registration as the default path:
 
 1. Extend `generate-rn-icons.js` to generate:
    - `icon-manifest.generated.ts`: canonical base-name -> size component map
    - `auto-register.generated.ts`: bootstrap that registers every generated icon at module init
    - `icon-names.generated.ts`: `ALL_ICON_NAMES` + `HomefieldIconName`
 2. Import/execute the generated auto-register bootstrap from the RN icon library entrypoint so consumers do not manually register.
 3. Keep legacy `registerIcons(...)` available only for backward compatibility (not required for normal usage).
 4. Type `HmfIcon` name with generated union to improve discoverability and compile-time safety.
 5. Add tests that lock in zero-setup behavior and generator correctness.
 
 ## Implementation todos
 1. **Define auto-registration contract**
    - Specify generated manifest shape and bootstrap execution order.
    - Guarantee idempotent registration and deterministic generated output.
 
 2. **Extend RN icon generator**
    - Update `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` to emit manifest/bootstrap/name-union files.
    - Ensure generated files are stable and CI-safe.
 
 3. **Wire zero-setup runtime path**
    - Hook generated bootstrap into package initialization.
    - Ensure `HmfIcon` resolves icons by name with no client registration code.
 
 4. **Strengthen types**
    - Apply generated `HomefieldIconName` to `HmfIcon` and exposed APIs where applicable.
 
 5. **Add comprehensive tests**
    - Runtime tests: render dynamic icon without any registration call.
    - Registry tests: idempotence, size resolution, and missing-name behavior.
    - Generator tests: expected generated contract and bootstrap content.
 
 6. **Update docs/migration guidance**
    - Replace manual registration guidance with zero-setup usage.
    - Add migration notes from legacy manual registration pattern.
 
 ## Test plan
 - Baseline: run existing affected tests before code changes.
 - After implementation:
   - run RN icon library tests,
   - run new runtime + generator tests,
   - run affected Nx targets for icon generation/prebuild.
 - Explicitly verify: `HmfIcon` works from clean consumer code path without calling `registerIcons(...)`.
 
 ## Risks and mitigations
 - **Startup/bundle overhead from auto-registering all icons**
   - Measure via existing build/test workflow; if needed, optimize generated bootstrap internals without requiring consumer-side manual mapping.
 - **Behavior regressions in existing consumers**
   - Preserve `registerIcons(...)` compatibility and add regression tests around existing API.
 - **Generator drift**
   - Add deterministic contract assertions for generated artifacts.
 
 ## Progress update
 - Repository write access and Node/Nx execution are currently blocked in this environment (`Permission denied and could not request permission from user`).
 - A ready-to-apply implementation patch has been prepared at:
   - `/Users/DKS0700123/.copilot/session-state/e1db69e0-275a-44b1-9c28-f0243f6db412/files/rn-icon-dx.patch`
 - The patch includes:
   - automatic icon registration module for RN (`auto-register.ts`),
   - `HmfIcon` integration + typed icon-name surface,
   - public API export updates,
   - tests for zero-setup behavior and idempotent registration,
   - docs/story updates removing manual `registerIcons(...)` setup.
 
 ## Next execution step once permissions are available
 1. Apply patch to repository.
    - Or run helper script: `/Users/DKS0700123/.copilot/session-state/e1db69e0-275a-44b1-9c28-f0243f6db412/files/rn-icon-dx-proposed/apply-rn-icon-dx.sh`
 2. Run validation targets:
    - `nx test hmf-react-native-icons`
    - `nx lint hmf-react-native-icons`
    - `nx build hmf-react-native-icons`
 3. Address any test/lint issues and finalize.
 
 ## Current execution status
 - Proposed implementation files are complete in the session artifact tree and include runtime auto-registration, API exports, tests, story, and docs updates.
 - Applying files into the workspace repo and running Nx validation remain blocked by environment permission policy (`Permission denied and could not request permission from user`).

1. {/* @format */}
2. 
3. import { Meta, Canvas } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks';
4. 
5. <Meta title="Development/React Native Icons/Contribution" />
6. 
7. # Contribution:
8. 
9. Contributing to the React Native icon library is straightforward.
10. 
11. 1. Add your approved SVG to `libs/icons/single-color` or `libs/icons/multi-color`.
12. 
13. 2. Regenerate the React Native icon components:
14. 
15. ```bash
16. npx nx run hmf-react-native-icons:prebuild
17. ```
18. 
19. This runs `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` and refreshes the generated icon exports.
20. For dynamic `HmfIcon` usage, you can either call `registerAllIcons()` once or use `registerIcons(...)` for tree-shaken bundles.
21.

 # Contribution:
 
 Contributing to the React Native icon library is straightforward.
 
 1. Add your approved SVG to `libs/icons/single-color` or `libs/icons/multi-color`.
 
 2. Regenerate the React Native icon components:
 
 ```bash
 npx nx run hmf-react-native-icons:prebuild
 ```
 
 This runs `tools/scripts/generate-rn-icons.js` and refreshes the generated icon exports.
 For dynamic `HmfIcon` usage, you can either call `registerAllIcons()` once or use `registerIcons(...)` for tree-shaken bundles.
